chunk_summary: Focuses on immunization and household counseling to prevent catabolic setbacks in high-risk cardiopulmonary children, integrating infection prevention into nutrition plans for measurable outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of routine immunization in reducing malnutrition risk in children with chronic cardiorespiratory diseases.
Learn strategies for household counseling to minimize infection exposure and improve nutrition outcomes.
Identify specific vaccination priorities, including annual influenza and pneumococcal vaccines, for high-risk children.
Develop illness plans to manage hydration and feeding during catabolic exacerbations.
key_facts: Routine immunization per India’s National Immunization Schedule is critical to prevent vaccine-preventable respiratory illnesses.
Annual influenza vaccination is recommended from 6 months of age for high-risk children and household contacts.
Pneumococcal vaccination is advised for children with chronic heart or lung disease based on local guidelines.
Dehydration episodes in cystic fibrosis require salt and fluid planning during hot seasons or illness.
Feeding intake below 75% of target for more than 24–48 hours warrants medical review.
reference_quote: Ensure routine immunization is complete per India’s National Immunization Schedule, because vaccine-preventable respiratory illness compounds malnutrition risk.
document_key_topics: Illness planning for hydration and feeding
